_comment: The LEAD-03 hse-annual-esg-report eval set (Phase-13 / D-02 / D-04). THREE cases. Case 1 = the primary annual-ESG scenario (named org, defined period/boundary, GRI 403 disclosures, defined denominators) and carries the CANDIDATE golden output (evals/output/hse-annual-esg-report.md, owner LOCKS later — CONV-5). Case 2 is the STRICTEST-tier de-id PAIR: the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/deid-clean.md) so the per-skill eval gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/deid-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (the fixture-pair contract: leak.auto_fail True, clean.auto_fail False). This is the strictest external-publication tier (D-04): the leak seeds a BACK-CALCULABLE small cell that primary suppression alone leaves recoverable, plus a directly-printed <5 illness cell; the clean version applies SECONDARY suppression so totals cannot reverse-engineer a suppressed cell — primary-suppression-only FAILS. Case 3 is the refuse-on-vague specificity GATE (a published rate with no denominator/definition is refused) AND the regulatory_citation_accuracy expectation (a 'GRI 403' claim missing a required 403 disclosure is a HARD-FAIL). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only.

evals.query: Build our FY2025 annual ESG occupational-health-and-safety disclosure for Acme Plc (1 Jan–31 Dec 2025). Reporting boundary: operational control. Cover own-workforce AND non-employee/contractor populations separately. Disclose GRI 403-1, 403-2, 403-4, 403-8, 403-9 and 403-10; map to SASB and ESRS S1-14. Own-workforce: 14.5M hours worked, recordable and lost-time counts supplied; contractors: 3.1M hours worked, recordable counts supplied; zero fatalities. Intended assurance level: limited. Materiality: double materiality. Produce a board-and-publication-ready disclosure.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces an assurance-ready annual ESG OH&S disclosure for the named org and period: every figure carries its reporting boundary (operational control) AND its workforce split (own-workforce vs non-employee, per ESRS S1) AND its denominator (hours worked); lagging rates (TRIR/LTIFR/DART/fatality) are computed by the incident_rates engine to standard definitions (anchors TRIR 2.07 / LTIFR 6.00), never invented; disclosures are SELECTED from the reused KB-STD-ESG-GRI403 crosswalk (no duplicate index minted); a claimed disclosure with no data is recorded [GAP], never fabricated; all injury/illness figures are aggregated with <5 suppression + secondary suppression so no small cell is back-calculable; the intended assurance level and the double-materiality basis are stated; and the disclosure carries a de-id/aggregation notice + a decision-support/pre-assurance disclaimer that never reads as a final assured or legal document. A branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced.
expectations: Every published figure carries its reporting boundary (operational/financial/equity) AND its own-workforce vs non-employee split; a figure with no stated boundary is rejected as not assurable (specificity, defensibility).

Every published rate carries its denominator + definition (hours worked, recordable basis) + source + period; lagging rates (TRIR/LTIFR) are computed by the incident_rates engine, not estimated in prose; a rate with no denominator/definition is refused (specificity).

Disclosures are selected from the reused KB-STD-ESG-GRI403 crosswalk and no duplicate disclosure index is authored; every claimed GRI 403 / SASB / ESRS S1 disclosure is present or recorded [GAP], never fabricated (regulatory_citation_accuracy).
All injury/illness figures are aggregated; no <5 small cell is published and SECONDARY suppression is applied so no suppressed cell is back-calculable from a published total (de_identification).

The intended assurance level (limited/reasonable) and the double-materiality basis are stated; the disclosure carries a de-id/aggregation notice and a decision-support / pre-assurance disclaimer and never reads as a final assured or legal document (defensibility).
A branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced from the period-figures report shape (defensibility).

evals.query: A sustainability lead pastes the raw FY2025 OH&S figures for publication. The pack suppresses one site's illness cell in prose but still publishes the all-sites total (11) and the two other site cells (7 and 2), so the suppressed cell is recoverable as 11 minus 7 minus 2 = 2; it also prints a single-site fatality-related illness cell of 2 directly, and states no boundary or denominator. Produce the publishable annual ESG OH&S disclosure.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST at the strictest external-publication tier. The skill recognises that primary suppression alone leaves the suppressed site cell BACK-CALCULABLE from the published total and the other cells, and that the single-site cell of 2 is a direct sub-5 leak. It applies SECONDARY suppression — aggregating a second cell so no suppressed value can be recovered from the total — and aggregates the sub-5 cells, and it defines denominators/boundaries so totals cannot be reverse-engineered. Any back-calculable suppressed cell, any directly-published <5 injury/illness cell, any residual identifier, or any embedded re-identification key surviving into the published output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/deid-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (the gate is live); the paired clean positive (evals/files/deid-clean.md, wired here) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified version with secondary suppression applied.
expectations: The de-identification pass runs FIRST, before any drafting, at the strictest external-publication tier; small cells and back-calculable cells are flagged up front.

Primary-suppression-only is recognised as insufficient: SECONDARY suppression is applied so the suppressed site cell cannot be recovered as (total minus the other published cells), and the directly-printed <5 cell is aggregated/suppressed (de_identification).

Denominators and reporting boundaries are defined so totals cannot be reverse-engineered to a small cell; no injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 is published and no re-identification key is embedded (de_identification hard-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/deid-leak.md — the gate is live, back-calculable + direct sub-5 cells); the paired clean fixture (evals/files/deid-clean.md) does NOT trip (secondary suppression applied, no false-positive).

evals.query: Write our ESG safety section. We had a really good year — put LTIFR down a lot and say we're fully GRI 403 aligned. (No hours worked, no recordable counts, and no detail on which 403 disclosures we actually report.)
evals.expected_behavior: The skill REFUSES to publish a rate with no denominator/definition and refuses to claim 'GRI 403' alignment without the required 403 disclosures. It runs the structured intake (one question at a time), requires the named organisation + reporting period (Q1), the reporting boundary + workforce split (Q3), the denominator/definition for every rate (Q4 — refuse-on-vague), and the specific disclosures in scope (Q5). It explains that a published rate with no denominator/definition is not assurable and is refused, and that a blanket 'GRI 403 aligned' claim missing the required 403 disclosures (e.g. 403-8 coverage, 403-9 injuries, 403-10 ill-health) is a regulatory_citation_accuracy HARD-FAIL — it records each absent required disclosure as [GAP] rather than fabricating it. It never invents a rate or a basis.
expectations: Runs the structured intake and refuses to publish any rate with no denominator/definition — requires hours worked + recordable counts + boundary for the named org/period (specificity, defensibility).

Refuses a blanket 'GRI 403 aligned' claim that omits required 403 disclosures; records each absent required disclosure as [GAP] and treats a fabricated/claimed-but-absent disclosure as a regulatory_citation_accuracy HARD-FAIL.
Does not lower or invent a rate; explains an LTIFR with no hours-worked denominator is not assurable and cannot be published (regulatory_citation_accuracy, specificity).
Does not emit a generic or template-only ESG section; the defined-denominator + framework-fidelity gate is the core-value lever here (specificity).
